verses:
namas tri⋅kāla⋅satyāya
jagannātha⋅sutāya ca
sa⋅bhṛtyāya sa⋅putrāya
sa⋅kalatrāya te namaḥ

vande śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅caitanyaṁ
bhaktānugraha⋅kātaram
jena kenāpi santuṣṭaṁ
bhakta⋅dattena śraddhayā

vande śrī kṛṣṇa⋅caitanyaṁ
bhagavantaṁ kṛpārṇavam
prema⋅bhakti⋅vitānārthaṁ
gauḍeṣv⋅avatatāra jaḥ

viśvambharāya gaurāya
caitanyāya mahātmane
śacī⋅putrāya mitrāya
lakṣmīśāya namo namaḥ

śrīman navadvīpa⋅kiśora⋅candra
hā nātha viśvambhara nāgarendra
hā śrī śacī⋅nandana citta⋅caura
prasīda he viṣṇupriyeśa gaura

ānanda⋅līlā⋅maya⋅vigrahāya
hemābha⋅divya⋅cchavi⋅sundarāya
tasmai mahā⋅prema⋅rasa⋅pradāya
caitanya⋅candrāya namo namas te

vairāgya⋅vidyā⋅nija⋅bhakti⋅yoga⋅
śikṣārtham ekaḥ puruṣaḥ purāṇaḥ
śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅caitanya⋅śarīra⋅dhārī
kṛpāmbudhir jas tam ahaṁ prapadye

jayati jayati devaḥ śrī⋅śacī⋅garbha⋅janma
jayati jayati bhakti⋅prema⋅dānaika⋅dharma
jayati jayati meruspardhi⋅gaurāṅga⋅dhāmā
jayati jayati dhanyaḥ kṛṣṇa⋅caitanya⋅nāmā

saṁsāra⋅duḥkha⋅jaladhau patitasya
kāma⋅krodhādi⋅nakra⋅makaraiḥ kavalī⋅kṛtasya
durvāsanā⋅nigaḍitasya nirāśrayasya
caitanya⋅candra mama dehi padāvalambam

anarpita⋅carīṁ cirāt karuṇayāvatīrṇaḥ kalau
samarpayitum unnatojjvala⋅rasāṁ sva⋅bhakti⋅śriyam
hariḥ puraṭa⋅sundara⋅dyuti⋅kadamba⋅sandīpitaḥ
sadā hṛdaya⋅kandare sphuratu vaḥ śacī⋅nandanaḥ

rādhā kṛṣṇa⋅praṇaya⋅vikṛtir hlādinī śaktir asmāt
ekātmānāv api bhuvi purā deha⋅bhedaṁ gatau tau
caitanyākhyaṁ prakaṭam adhunā tad⋅dvayaṁ caikyam āptaṁ
rādhā⋅bhāva⋅dyuti⋅suvalitaṁ naumi kṛṣṇa⋅svarūpam

śrī⋅rādhāyāḥ praṇaya⋅mahimā kīdṛśo vānayaivā⋅
svādyo jenādbhuta⋅madhurimā kīdṛśo vā madīyaḥ
saukhyaṁ cāsyā mad⋅anubhavataḥ kīdṛśaṁ veti lobhāt
tad⋅bhāvāḍhyaḥ samajani śacī⋅garbha⋅sindhau harīnduḥ
en_translation:
I offer my obeisances unto You, the son of Jagannātha Miśra, who are the Absolute Truth in the past, present, and future. I offer my respectful obeisances unto You, along with Your servants, Your followers, and Your associates.

I worship Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya, who is very eager to bestow mercy upon His devotees. He is satisfied by anything whatsoever offered by a devotee with faith.

I worship Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, an ocean of mercy, who descended in Bengal for the purpose of bestowing loving devotion.

Repeated obeisances to the maintainer of the universe, the golden Lord, the Supreme Soul, the son of Śacī, and the husband of the goddess of fortune.

O beautiful young moon of Navadvīpa! O Lord Viśvambhara, best among men! O son of Śacī, thief of my thoughts! O Lord of Viṣṇupriyā, please be pleased!

Repeated obeisances to You, whose form is full of joyful pastimes, who are beautiful with a divine golden luster, and who bestow the great mellow of divine love.

I surrender to the one ancient Supreme Person, an ocean of mercy, who—for the purpose of teaching renunciation and His own devotional service—assumed the form of Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya.

All glories to the Lord who was born from the womb of Śacī! All glories to Him whose only principle is to give devotional love! All glories to His golden form that rivals Mount Meru! Blessed is the name of Kṛṣṇa Caitanya!

O Lord Caitanya! I have fallen into the ocean of worldly miseries, swallowed up by the crocodiles and sharks of lust, anger, and greed. I am chained by bad desires and am without shelter. Please give me the support of Your feet.

May the son of Śacī, Lord Hari—resplendent with a cluster of beautiful golden effulgence—mercifully descend in this age to bestow the opulence of His own devotion in the most elevated and brilliant mellow, which was never given before. May He always manifest in the cave of your heart.

Rādhā is the transformation of Kṛṣṇa's love, His internal pleasure potency. Although They are one in nature, They formerly appeared on earth in two separate bodies. Now those two have attained unity as Caitanya. I offer obeisances to that form of Kṛṣṇa, who is covered by the mood and luster of Rādhā.

Out of a desire to know the glory of Rādhā's love, the wonderful sweetness She experiences through Me, and the happiness She derives from My experience, the moon-like Lord Hari was born from the womb of Śacī.
